description: Autonomous Campaign Sequences - Database schema, API routes, and worker logic for multi-step drip campaigns with delays, conditions, lead scoring, and stage progression. This is the core backend that powers rule-based lead warming without AI.
steps: STEP 1 - Prisma Schema Extension: Add new models to apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma: (a) `Sequence` model with fields: id, name, description, status (draft/active/paused/completed), leadGroupIds (String[]), triggerType (manual/on_lead_created/on_tag_added), triggerConfig (Json), pauseOnReply (Boolean default true), sendingWindowStart (String? like '09:00'), sendingWindowEnd (String? like '18:00'), dailyLimit (Int?), timezone (String default 'UTC'), createdAt, updatedAt. (b) `SequenceStep` model with: id, sequenceId, position (Int), type (enum: send_email/send_whatsapp/delay/condition/update_lead_stage/update_lead_score), config (Json - holds templateId, delayHours, condition rules, score delta, target stage), nextStepId (String?), conditionTrueStepId (String?), conditionFalseStepId (String?), createdAt, updatedAt. (c) `SequenceEnrollment` model with: id, sequenceId, leadId, status (enum: active/paused/completed/exited), currentStepId (String?), nextRunAt (DateTime?), startedAt, completedAt, exitReason (String?), createdAt, updatedAt. (d) `SequenceStepExecution` model with: id, enrollmentId, stepId, status (enum: pending/completed/failed/skipped), executedAt (DateTime?), result (Json?), createdAt. (e) Add `LeadStage` enum: new/contacted/engaged/warm/hot/converted/lost. Add `stage` field (LeadStage default 'new') and `score` field (Int default 0 - already exists) to Lead model. (f) Create migration file at apps/api/prisma/migrations/20260526000000_add_sequences/migration.sql.

STEP 2 - Shared Types and Schemas: Add to packages/shared/src/types/index.ts: SEQUENCE_STATUS, SEQUENCE_STEP_TYPE, SEQUENCE_ENROLLMENT_STATUS, LEAD_STAGE constants and types. Add to packages/shared/src/schemas/index.ts: sequenceSchema, sequenceStepSchema with Zod validation. Export all new types.

STEP 3 - Sequence API Routes: Create apps/api/src/routes/sequences.ts following the pattern in automations.ts. Endpoints: GET /api/sequences (list with pagination), GET /api/sequences/:id (detail with steps and enrollment stats), POST /api/sequences (create with steps), PUT /api/sequences/:id (update), DELETE /api/sequences/:id, POST /api/sequences/:id/activate (set status active, enroll leads from groups), POST /api/sequences/:id/pause, POST /api/sequences/:id/resume, GET /api/sequences/:id/enrollments (paginated list of enrolled leads with status), POST /api/sequences/:id/enroll (manually enroll specific leadIds), POST /api/sequences/:id/unenroll (remove leads). Register route in apps/api/src/index.ts.

STEP 4 - Sequence Execution Worker: Create apps/api/src/services/sequence-engine.ts. Functions: (a) enrollLeadsInSequence(sequenceId, leadIds[]) - creates SequenceEnrollment records, sets currentStepId to first step, calculates nextRunAt. (b) processSequenceStep(enrollmentId) - the core step processor: fetches enrollment+step, checks if in sending window, executes step based on type (send_email: render template via Handlebars and queue message; send_whatsapp: same for whatsapp; delay: just update nextRunAt; condition: evaluate using condition-evaluator pattern (e.g., lead.score > 50, message.opened == true) and branch; update_lead_stage: update lead.stage; update_lead_score: increment/decrement lead.score). After step execution, advance to nextStepId or complete enrollment. (c) checkForReplies(enrollmentId) - if sequence.pauseOnReply is true and lead has inbound message since enrollment started, pause the enrollment with exitReason='replied'. (d) tickSequences() - called every 1 minute by cron: find all SequenceEnrollments where status='active' AND nextRunAt <= now, process each one.

STEP 5 - Lead Scoring and Stage Progression Rules: Create apps/api/src/services/lead-scoring.ts. Functions: (a) updateLeadScore(leadId, event: 'open'|'click'|'reply'|'bounce', delta: number) - updates lead.score. Default deltas: open=+5, click=+10, reply=+25, bounce=-10. (b) evaluateStageProgression(leadId) - checks score thresholds and auto-advances stage: score >= 10 -> contacted, score >= 30 -> engaged, score >= 50 -> warm, score >= 80 -> hot. Updates lead.stage if changed, publishes event via event-bus. (c) Wire into existing webhook handlers: when email.opened event comes in (from SendGrid/SMTP webhooks in apps/api/src/routes/webhooks.ts), call updateLeadScore with 'open'. Same for click, reply, bounce.

STEP 6 - Sequence Queue and Cron: Add 'sequence-queue' to apps/api/src/queue/index.ts (or extend existing pattern). Create a cron job (setInterval 60s like campaign scheduler) that calls tickSequences(). Wire the sequence step processor as a BullMQ worker. Add deduplication check: never send same template to same lead twice within a sequence.

STEP 7 - Sequence Analytics Endpoint: Add to sequences route: GET /api/sequences/:id/analytics - returns per-step metrics (how many enrollments reached each step, how many completed, failed, conversion rate per step). Also add GET /api/analytics/sequences to the analytics route for overview of all sequences.

STEP 8 - Tests: Create apps/api/src/routes/sequences.test.ts (follow pattern in automations.test.ts - mock prisma, test CRUD + activate/enroll). Create apps/api/src/services/sequence-engine.test.ts (test step processing logic, delay calculation, condition branching, reply pause). Create apps/api/src/services/lead-scoring.test.ts (test score updates and stage progression).
acceptance_criteria: Running `npx vitest run` in apps/api passes all existing 414+ tests plus new sequence and lead-scoring tests
TypeScript compiles without errors: `npx tsc --noEmit` in apps/api succeeds
Sequence CRUD endpoints follow the same response format as other routes ({ data, meta })
Lead model has stage field with proper enum and default value
Sequence enrollment lifecycle works: enroll -> process steps -> advance -> complete
pauseOnReply logic correctly stops sequence when lead replies
Lead scoring updates score and auto-advances stage based on thresholds
Deduplication prevents same template being sent twice to same lead in a sequence
verification: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in apps/api to verify TypeScript compilation
Run `npx vitest run` in apps/api to verify all tests pass
Run `npx prisma generate` in apps/api to verify schema is valid
